findings.summary: 4-Hydroxyisoleucine, the principal insulinotropic amino acid from Trigonella foenum-graecum seeds, directly stimulates pancreatic beta-cell insulin secretion and improves peripheral glucose uptake via insulin-receptor sensitisation; when combined with exogenous insulin, additive blood-glucose lowering leading to hypoglycaemia has been documented in clinical reports.
findings.citation: Broca et al., Am J Physiol Endocrinol Metab 1999; case reports summarized

findings.summary: A randomised controlled trial in type 2 diabetes patients on insulin found that adjunctive fenugreek seed powder (10 g/day for 8 weeks) reduced fasting blood glucose by an additional 16 mg/dL and significantly increased hypoglycaemic episodes compared to insulin alone, particularly in the early morning.

The galactomannan fibre fraction of fenugreek slows carbohydrate absorption and blunts postprandial glucose excursions; this is beneficial in unmedicated hyperglycaemia but can cause dangerous hypoglycaemia when the patient is simultaneously receiving long-acting insulin without dose adjustment.
findings.citation: Sowmya & Rajyalakshmi, Plant Foods Hum Nutr 1999
